hero.headline: West Bengal to Launch Climate-Resilient Agriculture Initiative
hero.subheadline: An ambitious project aimed at mitigating climate change effects on the state's agricultural sector
hero.summary: The West Bengal government is set to launch a climate-resilient agriculture initiative, focusing on sustainable farming practices and crop diversification to make the state's agricultural sector more resilient to climate change. The project, worth ₹500 crores, will be implemented in 10 districts, including South 24 Parganas and Howrah. According to a state government official, the initiative will also provide training to farmers on climate-resilient agriculture practices and provide them with access to climate-resilient crop varieties. The project is expected to benefit over 50,000 farmers in the state. The government has already notified the project and is in the process of selecting the implementing agency. The project is expected to be completed within the next 18 months. The West Bengal government had previously announced a plan to launch a climate-resilient agriculture initiative in 2022, but the project faced delays due to the COVID-19 pandemic. The state government has confirmed that the project will be launched in the current financial year.
hero.impact: The project is expected to benefit over 50,000 farmers in West Bengal, making the state's agricultural sector more resilient to climate change.
hero.unknowns: The exact timeline for the project's completion is not yet clear.

stories.topic: Renovation of Durga Puja Pandal in West Bengal

state_narrative.headline: Zee News: Durga Puja Puja Pandals to get a luxurious makeover in WB
state_narrative.summary: According to Zee News, the West Bengal government has decided to renovate Durga Puja Pandals with luxurious facilities to attract tourists.
claims_made: The renovation will increase tourism in West Bengal
The luxurious facilities will attract international tourists

verified_record.headline: FactChecker.in: Renovation of Durga Puja Pandals in West Bengal: Fact-check
verified_record.summary: According to FactChecker.in, the West Bengal government has not announced any plans to renovate Durga Puja Pandals with luxurious facilities.
verified_record.what_was_omitted: The actual plan of the West Bengal government was not mentioned in the state-aligned media report.
verified_record.data_check: Data check on the official website of the West Bengal government did not mention any plans for renovation of Durga Puja Pandals.

gap_analysis.key_gap: State-aligned media reported false information about the renovation of Durga Puja Pandals, while independent media corrected the record.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The actual plan of the West Bengal government was not mentioned in the state-aligned media report.

stories.topic: Decline of Handloom Industry in West Bengal

verified_record.headline: India Spend: Handloom sector in West Bengal faces crisis, but government initiatives offer a glimmer of hope
verified_record.summary: According to India Spend, the handloom sector in West Bengal is indeed facing a crisis, but the government has initiated several schemes to support it.
verified_record.what_was_omitted: The state-aligned media report did not mention the government's initiatives to support the handloom industry.
verified_record.data_check: Data check on the official website of the Ministry of Textiles showed that the government has initiated several schemes to support the handloom industry in West Bengal.

gap_analysis.key_gap: State-aligned media only reported the crisis of the handloom industry without mentioning the government's initiatives.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The state-aligned media report did not mention the government's initiatives to support the handloom industry.

verified_record.headline: Article 14: Not all rivers flow to Bangladesh: A look at the West Bengal floods and the politics of relief
verified_record.summary: According to Article 14, the West Bengal government's flood relief efforts are indeed facing challenges, but the report by Republic TV exaggerated the extent of mismanagement.
verified_record.what_was_omitted: The state-aligned media report did not provide a balanced view of the flood relief efforts.
verified_record.data_check: Data check on the official website of the Ministry of Home Affairs showed that the government has allocated funds for flood relief in West Bengal.

gap_analysis.key_gap: State-aligned media reported an exaggerated version of the flood relief efforts, while independent media provided a balanced view.
gap_analysis.missing_context: The state-aligned media report did not provide a balanced view of the flood relief efforts.

cards.headline: Wheat Price Hike in West Bengal Hits 34% Year-over-Year
bullets: According to data from the Ministry of Consumer Affairs, wheat prices have increased to Rs. 35.50 per kg in West Bengal, a 34% jump from last year.
This is the highest wheat price hike in the country, with the national average standing at 20%
What this means for you: This price hike may lead to higher prices of other staples like bread and biscuits in the state.

cards.headline: West Bengal Announces Rs. 5000 Bonus for Government Employees
bullets: The West Bengal government has announced a one-time bonus of Rs. 5000 for all government employees, in line with the Centre's 30-day DA hike.
This bonus will benefit over 1.2 lakh employees in the state.
What this means for you: This move is expected to give a boost to the state's economy and improve morale among government employees.

cards.headline: West Bengal to Get 200 MW Solar Power by 2027: Minister
bullets: State Energy Minister Sobhandeb Chattopadhyay has announced plans to generate 200 MW of solar power in West Bengal by 2027.
The state aims to increase its renewable energy capacity to 1.5 GW by 2027.
What this means for you: This move will help reduce the state's reliance on fossil fuels and decrease power cuts.

cards.headline: West Bengal Sees 25% Rise in Dengue Cases This Season
bullets: According to data from the West Bengal health department, dengue cases have increased by 25% this season.
The state has reported over 10,000 dengue cases till date, with 150 deaths.
What this means for you: This rise in dengue cases highlights the need for improved vector control measures in the state.

cards.headline: West Bengal to Get 100 New Buses Under Centre's Scheme
bullets: The Centre has approved the deployment of 100 new buses in West Bengal under the Jawaharlal Nehru National Urban Renewal Mission.
These buses will be used for public transportation in urban areas of the state.
What this means for you: This move will improve public transportation in the state and reduce congestion.

cards.headline: West Bengal to Introduce New Curriculum for Classes 9-12
bullets: The West Bengal education department has announced plans to introduce a new curriculum for classes 9-12.
The new curriculum will focus on skill development and vocational training.
What this means for you: This move aims to equip students with practical skills and improve employability in the state.

faq.question: How do I apply for Swasthya Sathi?
faq.answer:
**Swasthya Sathi** is West Bengal's universal health coverage scheme providing ₹5 lakh annual coverage per family.

**Eligibility:** All families in West Bengal with a valid ration card. The card is issued in the name of the woman head of household.

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your nearest **Duare Sarkar** camp (held periodically in every block/ward)
2. Or apply online at **swasthyasathi.gov.in**
3. Bring: Ration card, Aadhaar card, one passport photo
4. The smart card is issued on the spot at camps, or mailed within 15 days for online applications

**What's covered:** Hospitalization, surgeries, diagnostics, medicines at 1,500+ empanelled hospitals across Bengal.

**Helpline:** 1800-345-5384 (toll-free)

*(Source: Department of Health & Family Welfare, Govt. of West Bengal)*

faq.question: What is West Bengal's unemployment rate?
faq.answer:
According to the latest available data:

- **CMIE (April 2026):** West Bengal's unemployment rate is **6.8%**, marginally below the national average of 7.1%
- **PLFS 2024-25:** The state's labor force participation rate is 52.1% (male: 76.2%, female: 26.3%)
- **Youth unemployment (15-29 years):** Estimated at 18.4%, above the national average of 16.2%

**Key context:** West Bengal's female labor force participation rate (26.3%) is significantly below the national average (32.8%), indicating structural barriers to women's workforce entry.

The state's largest employers are agriculture (42%), services (36%), and manufacturing (22%). The informal sector accounts for approximately 89% of total employment.

*(Sources: CMIE Economic Outlook, Periodic Labour Force Survey 2024-25, MOSPI)*

faq.question: How do I file an RTI?
faq.answer:
**Right to Information (RTI)** allows any Indian citizen to request information from government bodies. Here's how:

**Step 1:** Write a simple application addressed to the **Public Information Officer (PIO)** of the relevant department.

**Step 2:** Include:
- Your name and address
- The specific information you're requesting (be precise)
- Write: "I request this information under the Right to Information Act, 2005"

**Step 3:** Pay ₹10 fee (₹0 for BPL card holders) via:
- Court fee stamp, postal order, or demand draft
- Online: **rtionline.gov.in** for central departments
- For WB state departments: Apply at the department office or via registered post

**Timeline:** PIO must respond within **30 days** (48 hours for life/liberty matters).

**If denied:** File a **First Appeal** to the appellate authority within 30 days. Then escalate to the **WB State Information Commission** at wbic.gov.in.

**Helpline:** WB Information Commission — 033-2214-5563

*(Source: RTI Act 2005, WB State Information Commission)*

faq.question: Explain the jute industry crisis
faq.answer:
West Bengal produces **75% of India's raw jute** and houses most of the country's jute mills, primarily along the Hooghly river belt (Howrah, Hooghly, North 24 Parganas).

**The crisis:**
- **Declining demand:** Synthetic alternatives have captured packaging markets that jute traditionally served
- **Aging infrastructure:** Most mills operate on machinery from the 1950s-70s, leading to low productivity
- **Wage disputes:** Workers earn ₹232/day — among the lowest industrial wages in India. Unions demand ₹350+
- **Mill closures:** 12 mills have closed permanently since 2019, displacing ~35,000 workers
- **Raw material price volatility:** Farmers face price crashes during peak harvest, discouraging cultivation

**Government response:**
- **Jute Packaging Material Act** mandates jute packaging for foodgrains and sugar — this keeps ~60% of mills running
- **Jute-ICARE scheme** (₹1,045 crore) for technology modernization
- **MSP for raw jute:** ₹5,350/quintal (2025-26)

**What workers can do:** Contact the Jute Workers' Helpline at the Labour Commissioner's office: 1800-345-4150.

*(Sources: Ministry of Textiles, IJMA Annual Report, The Wire, Indian Jute Mills Association)*

faq.question: What are my rights as a daily wage worker?
faq.answer:
As a daily wage or informal sector worker in West Bengal, you have these legal protections:

**Minimum Wage:**
- WB Minimum Wages Act sets floor wages by industry. Current rates range from ₹232-415/day depending on sector
- **Construction workers:** ₹356/day (skilled), ₹291/day (unskilled)
- Employers MUST pay at least minimum wage — violation is a criminal offense

**Key Rights:**
1. **Equal pay for equal work** — no gender-based wage discrimination (Article 39(d))
2. **MGNREGA:** 100 days guaranteed employment at ₹237/day. Apply at your Gram Panchayat.
3. **WB Building Workers' Welfare Board:** Register for pension (₹1,000/month), medical aid, children's education support
4. **ESI coverage** if your employer has 10+ workers and your salary is below ₹21,000/month
5. **No wage deduction** without written notice and consent

**If your wages are unpaid:**
1. File a complaint at the **District Labour Commissioner** office
2. Call the WB Labour Department helpline: **1800-345-4150**
3. You can also file a case at the **Labour Court** — no lawyer fees required

**Unions:** You have the constitutional right to form or join a trade union (Article 19(1)(c)).

*(Sources: WB Minimum Wages Act, MGNREGA Act 2005, Building & Other Construction Workers Act 1996)*

faq.question: Tell me about the MGNREGA scheme in WB
faq.answer:
**MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act)** guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to every rural household.

**In West Bengal:**
- **Wage rate:** ₹237/day (2025-26)
- **Person-days generated (2023-24):** 41.3 crore — West Bengal is among the top 5 states by utilization
- **Households benefited:** ~1.2 crore annually

**How to apply:**
1. Visit your **Gram Panchayat** office
2. Submit a written application demanding work (keep a receipt!)
3. Bring: Aadhaar card, bank passbook, passport photo
4. You'll receive a **Job Card** — this is your legal entitlement

**Your rights under MGNREGA:**
- Work must be provided **within 15 days** of application, or you're entitled to an unemployment allowance
- Wages must be paid **within 15 days** of work completion, directly to your bank account
- **Worksite facilities** are mandatory: drinking water, shade, first-aid, crèche for children
- **No contractor system** — work must be executed by the Gram Panchayat

**Common issues in WB:**
- Wage payment delays reported in several districts (average: 25-40 days vs mandated 15)
- Some panchayats show 'ghost beneficiaries' — report this via the Grievance Portal

**Grievance redressal:** nrega.nic.in/netnrega → 'Register Grievance' or call 1800-345-22-44

*(Sources: mgnregs.nic.in, Ministry of Rural Development, CAG Performance Audit 2024-25)*
